ddls:
CREATE TABLE "respondents" (
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each respondent */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the respondent */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the respondent */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the respondent */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the respondent */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group of the respondent (e.g., 20-24 years) */,
  "avg_hours_worked_per_week" REAL /* Average hours worked per week by the respondent */,
  "willing_to_work_extra" INTEGER /* Whether the respondent is willing to work extra hours */,
  "willing_to_work_extra_hours" INTEGER /* Number of extra hours the respondent is willing to work */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the respondent joined the platform */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the respondent's profile was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "skills_learned" (
  "skill_learned_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each skill learned */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who learned the skill */,
  "how_learned" TEXT /* Method by which the respondent learned the skill (e.g., College, On-the-Job) */,
  "year_learned" INTEGER /* Year when the skill was learned */,
  "institute" TEXT /* Name of the institute or organization where the skill was learned */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Certification or degree obtained for the skill */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("skill_learned_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_skills_learned_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "freelancing_experience" (
  "experience_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each experience entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent with the experience */,
  "years_of_experience" TEXT /* Number of years the respondent has been freelancing (e.g., 3-5 years) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the freelancing experience */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the freelancing experience (if applicable) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the freelancing experience */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experience_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_freelancing_experience_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "programming_languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who knows the language */,
  "language" TEXT /* Programming language the respondent is comfortable with (e.g., Kotlin, Haskell) */,
  "proficiency_level" TEXT /* Proficiency level in the programming language (e.g., Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced) */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Years of experience with the programming language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_programming_languages_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "in_demand_languages" (
  "demand_language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each in-demand language entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who thinks the language is in demand */,
  "language" TEXT /* Programming language the respondent thinks is in high demand (e.g., VC++, Ruby) */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason why the respondent thinks the language is in high demand */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demand_language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_in_demand_languages_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "front_end_frameworks" (
  "framework_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each framework entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who knows the framework */,
  "framework" TEXT /* Front-end framework the respondent is good at (e.g., JSP, Highlight.js) */,
  "proficiency_level" TEXT /* Proficiency level in the front-end framework */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Years of experience with the front-end framework */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("framework_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_front_end_frameworks_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "in_demand_frameworks" (
  "demand_framework_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each in-demand framework entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who thinks the framework is in demand */,
  "framework" TEXT /* Front-end framework the respondent thinks is in high demand (e.g., Polymer, ASP.NET) */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason why the respondent thinks the framework is in high demand */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demand_framework_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_in_demand_frameworks_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "databases" (
  "database_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each database entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who knows the database */,
  "database" TEXT /* Database the respondent is most comfortable with (e.g., CouchDB, Oracle 12c) */,
  "proficiency_level" TEXT /* Proficiency level in the database */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Years of experience with the database */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("database_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_databases_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "in_demand_databases" (
  "demand_database_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each in-demand database entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who thinks the database is in demand */,
  "database" TEXT /* Database the respondent thinks is in high demand (e.g., Redis, Cassandra) */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason why the respondent thinks the database is in high demand */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demand_database_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_in_demand_databases_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_matching" (
  "job_match_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job match */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent matched with the job */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job matched with the respondent */,
  "matched_date" TEXT /* Date when the job was matched */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the job match (e.g., Pending, Accepted, Rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_match_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_matching_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_matching_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Description of the job */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the job is based */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the job */,
  "posted_date" TEXT /* Date when the job was posted */,
  "deadline" TEXT /* Deadline for applying to the job */,
  "required_skills" TEXT /* Skills required for the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "updates_with_new_technologies" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who updates with new technologies */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the respondent updates with new technologies (e.g., Once a month, Once in 2 years) */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Date of the last update */,
  "next_update_planned" TEXT /* Date of the next planned update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_updates_with_new_technologies_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_spent_learning_new_technologies" (
  "learning_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each learning entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who spends time learning new technologies */,
  "hours_spent" TEXT /* Number of hours the respondent spends learning new technologies (e.g., 9-10 hours, 3-4 hours) */,
  "last_learning_date" TEXT /* Date of the last learning session */,
  "next_learning_planned" TEXT /* Date of the next planned learning session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("learning_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_time_spent_learning_new_technologies_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_satisfaction" (
  "satisfaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each satisfaction entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who has a satisfaction level */,
  "satisfaction_level" TEXT /* Level of satisfaction the respondent has with their freelancing job (e.g., Not sure, Satisfied, Most satisfied) */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the satisfaction level */,
  "date_surveyed" TEXT /* Date when the satisfaction level was surveyed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("satisfaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_satisfaction_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)
